prompt:
Repair the interrupted customer-name migration in `customer_names.migration`. The database begins with one required `customers.name` field and must move through an expand/backfill/contract rollout to required `given_name` and `family_name` fields. The supplied repository API must remain usable in every phase: legacy display-name callers and structured-name callers are both accepted, expanded-schema writes keep both representations synchronized, and reads fall back to the legacy value until structured data exists. Name parsing is deterministic: normalize whitespace, use the final word as the family name, and represent a mononym with an empty family name.

The focused defect is in `backfill_batch`: it durably advances the migration checkpoint before the corresponding customer updates commit. A deterministic failure injected partway through a batch therefore rolls the data back but leaves the checkpoint ahead, so a retry skips customers. Make each batch atomic across its structured-name updates, checkpoint, and completion marker. On any exception, both customer data and migration progress must roll back; retrying the same batch must process the same customer IDs. Preserve positive bounded batch sizes, ascending-ID checkpoints, idempotent reruns, and rows already written by dual-write clients.

Keep the rest of the migration contract intact. `expand_schema` and `contract_schema` are idempotent; contract is forbidden until backfill is complete and every structured name is present; the contracted table removes `name`; repository behavior survives the contract; and the versioned rollback snapshot can restore the exact legacy IDs, emails, and display names from either expanded or contracted state. Use only SQLite and the Python standard library. Do not add network access, wall-clock dependencies, randomness, sleeps, or subprocesses. Do not modify `protected_tests/` or `fixtures/`. Success is `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest discover -s protected_tests -p 'test_*.py' -v` passing from the repository root.
verify_cmd: env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest discover -s protected_tests -p 'test_*.py' -v
